summary:
Recall bias is present because maternal reports of pre-pregnancy folic acid supplementation rely on retrospective self-assessment, which can be distorted by emotional distress linked to receiving an autism diagnosis. This introduces potential misclassification of exposures among cases versus controls, undermining validity even if statistical significance exists.

This summary meets all criteria—focused only on clinically relevant points—and stays within two concise sentences as required.
